residual_gaps_for_builder_applied: G008: SbHd1 consolidated with SbCO; E092 (SbHd1->SbGhd7) DROPPED as rice-only mechanism (Hd1-Ghd7 complex) without sorghum-direct evidence.
G005: SbPhyA and SbCRY1 DROPPED from model (no downstream edges; option (a) per judge recommendation).
G011: SbGI has 1 curated inhibitor (CCA1) and 5 outgoing edges; flagged literature_gap for missing TOC1->GI and EC->GI edges.
G014: SbFT13 NOT included (Wolabu 2016 reports non-detectable in tested tissues).
G003_residual: EC edges (E096/E097/E098/E099/E100/E106/E111) retained in v1 with original confidence levels; rice ortholog inference noted in edge mechanisms.
motifs_applied: Perception Gate: Light -> PHYB -> PRR37 / GHD7 (no Light bypass to PRR37/GHD7/FT/Flowering_Time)
Multi-Output Scaffold: PRR37 -> (FT1, FT8, FT10, EHD1, CO); GHD7 -> (FT1, FT8, FT10, EHD1, ELF3, FKF1); ID1 -> 6 targets
Coherent Feed-Forward: PRR37 -| FT + PRR37 -> GHD7 -| EHD1 -> FT; ELF3 -| both PRR37 and GHD7
Mutual Inhibition: CCA1 -| TOC1 -| CCA1 oscillator; GHD7 -| ELF3 / ELF3 -| GHD7 daylength switch
Age-pathway convergence: miR156h -| SBP -> miR172a -| AP2; miR156h -| SBP4 -> SOC1
metadata.bypass_policy: Light NOT connected directly to PRR37/GHD7/FT/Flowering_Time. Photoperiod_SD DROPPED (direct FT activation bypass). Photoperiod_LD retained as coincidence-regime input to PRR37 and GHD7 (separate from raw Light).

nodes.description: Environmental light input; activates photoreceptors PHYB/PHYC and entrains the morning clock (CCA1/LHY).
Blue light input to the LOV-domain F-box protein FKF1.
Binary/continuous LD regime indicator: under LD the evening light window coincides with PRR37/GHD7 transcriptional peaks.
Bioactive GA pool (GA1/GA20); mild flowering-promoting hormone in cereals. Encoded as a source hormone; exogenous GA3 treatment maps to exogenous_supply.
Red-light photoreceptor; primary photoperiod sensor. ma3^R null causes ~60-day-earlier LD flowering (Childs 1997, Yang 2014). Ma-locus alias: Ma3.
Secondary red-light photoreceptor; acts in parallel with PHYB to activate PRR37/GHD7. Ma-locus alias: Ma5.
Morning-phased MYB transcription factor; light-entrained at dawn; represses TOC1, GI, and PRR37.
Morning-phased MYB transcription factor partially redundant with CCA1; represses TOC1.
Evening-phased PRR protein; core oscillator component that represses CCA1 (negative feedback loop).
Afternoon/evening-phased clock protein; activates PRR37, CO, and EHD1 as part of the photoperiod-sensing module. sbgi-ems1 delays flowering ~30 days.
LOV-domain F-box protein activated by blue light; co-stabilises CO with GI in the late afternoon. Repressed by GHD7 (Tadesse 2024).
Evening Complex scaffold; represses PRR37 and GHD7 (rice ortholog evidence). Inactivated post-translationally by PHYB. Reciprocally repressed by GHD7.
Evening Complex subunit that assembles with LUX onto ELF3 docking protein (Nusinow 2011); required for EC activity.
Evening Complex DNA-binding subunit that targets PRR/Ghd7 promoters (rice OsLUX evidence; Andrade 2022).
Key LD floral repressor; directly represses EHD1 and florigens (FT1/FT8/FT10), and activates GHD7 and CO. Dominant Ma1 gives latest flowering. Ma-locus alias: Ma1.

nodes.full_name: Grain number, plant height and heading date 7 (Ma6 locus, Sobic.006G004400)
nodes.description: Major LD floral repressor; directly represses EHD1, FT1/FT8/FT10, ELF3, FKF1 (Tadesse 2024 ChIP-seq). Ma-locus alias: Ma6.
Enhances amplitude of PRR37 and CO transcription in LD. ma2 (L141*) flowers ~100 days earlier. Source node: no curated upstream regulator.

nodes.full_name: INDETERMINATE 1 (conserved maize ID1 / rice Ehd2 ortholog)
nodes.description: Master regulator of sorghum flowering; activates CO, PRR37, GHD7, EHD1 and the florigens. sbid1-2 (Q199*) delays flowering ~170 days. Source node (no curated upstream regulator).
B-box TF; activator of EHD1 and florigens in permissive backgrounds. Stabilised by FKF1-GI; destabilised by PHYB in LD daylight (E095). SbHd1 is treated as alias of CO (Judge G008).
B-type response regulator; primary direct activator of FT1/FT8/FT10. Repressed by PRR37 and GHD7 in LD.
Mobile florigen; forms florigen activation complex with FD1 and GF14 at the SAM. Directly repressed by PHYB even under PRR37/GHD7-null background.
Principal florigen in sorghum; binds GF14 (not FD1) for FAC. Activates SOC1 at the SAM.
Major florigen; directly repressed by GHD7 (Tadesse 2024 ChIP-seq). Binds GF14.

nodes.full_name: FLOWERING LOCUS T 12 (FlrAvgD1, Sb06g012260, panicoid-specific PEBP)
nodes.description: Floral suppressor; loss-of-function drove photoperiod-insensitive temperate adaptation (Cuevas 2016). Source node — no curated upstream regulator.
bZIP transcription factor; binds FT1 at the SAM to assemble the florigen activation complex (FAC) and activate AP1/SOC1.

nodes.full_name: General Regulatory Factor 14 (14-3-3 scaffold; Sb14-3-3 composite)
nodes.description: 14-3-3 scaffold; required component of the FAC with FT1/FT8/FT10 (Cai 2016, Wolabu 2016). Promotes flowering.

nodes.full_name: SUPPRESSOR OF OVEREXPRESSION OF CO 1 (SbMADS31, Sobic.004G003434)
nodes.description: MADS-box flowering integrator at the SAM; activated by florigens, SBP4, and LFY/AP1 feedback.
Meristem-identity integrator; activated by SOC1 and activates AP1 in a mutual reinforcement loop.
Terminal meristem-identity MADS-box; commits shoot apical meristem to floral fate.
AP2-like floral repressor; targeted for cleavage by miR172a in the age pathway. Represses FT8 and directly delays flowering.
Age-pathway SPL TF; targeted by miR156h; activates miR172a transcription (age-pathway amplifier).
Age-pathway SPL TF; targeted by miR156h; activates SOC1 at the SAM (age-pathway convergence with photoperiod pathway).
Age-pathway microRNA; high at juvenile phase, declines with age. Cleaves SBP19 and SBP4 transcripts. Source node — intrinsic age program.
Age-pathway microRNA; activated by SBP19 upon decline of miR156h. Cleaves AP2 transcripts to relieve flowering repression.
Days to flowering / heading in sorghum. Higher value = later flowering. Florigens (FT1/FT8/FT10), SOC1, AP1, GF14, and Gibberellin reduce days (sign=-1); FT12 and AP2 delay flowering (sign=+1).

edges.mechanism: Red light activates phytochrome B photoreceptor; Pfr form accumulates in light.

evidence.title: The sorghum photoperiod sensitivity gene, Ma3, encodes a phytochrome B
evidence.authors: Childs KL, Miller FR, Cordonnier-Pratt MM, Pratt LH, Morgan PW, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: ma3 sorghum lacks a 123-KD phytochrome that predominates in light-grown plants and that is present in non-ma3 plants.
evidence.claim: Light activates PhyB (Ma3) expression / Pfr accumulation

edges.mechanism: Phytochrome C is activated by red light; Ma5 locus encodes SbPhyC.

evidence.title: Deleterious mutations predicted in the sorghum Maturity and Dwarf genes from whole-genome resequencing
evidence.authors: Thurber CS, Ma JM, Higgins RH, Brown PJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma5 = SbPhyC (Sobic.001G087100, Chr01)
evidence.claim: Ma5 locus encodes Phytochrome C, a light photoreceptor

edges.mechanism: Dawn light entrains the morning oscillator: CCA1 expression peaks at dawn and is light-gated. Conservation across Arabidopsis and grasses (rice OsCCA1).

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: LHY, CCA1, TOC1 (circadian oscillator)...entrained by light-dark cycles.
evidence.claim: Light entrains the sorghum circadian clock at dawn, driving CCA1 morning peak

edges.mechanism: Dawn light activates LHY morning expression alongside CCA1 in the core oscillator. Light entrainment is a canonical clock input.

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: LHY, CCA1, TOC1 (circadian oscillator)...entrained by light-dark cycles.
evidence.claim: Light entrains LHY morning expression in sorghum

edges.mechanism: SbFKF1/SbFFL contains a LOV domain that forms a covalent adduct with FMN upon blue-light illumination, activating the F-box protein to recruit substrates via SCF ubiquitin ligase.

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbGI physically interacts with the sorghum FKF1-like (SbFFL) protein, and the SbGI-SbFFL interaction is stimulated by blue light.
evidence.claim: Blue light activates the SbFKF1-SbGI complex formation

edges.mechanism: Long-day light evening phase coincides with SbPRR37 evening mRNA peak via PhyB; in SD the evening peak is eliminated because the phase occurs in darkness.

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: peaks of SbPRR37 mRNA abundance in the morning and in the evening...evening peak of SbPRR37 expression in SD does not occur due to darkness.
evidence.claim: LD photoperiod activates SbPRR37 evening peak

edges.mechanism: LD induces evening peak of SbGhd7 via PhyB; SD eliminates this peak (darkness).

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days by Activating Expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: evening peak of expression is attenuated in SD when this phase occurs in darkness.
evidence.claim: LD photoperiod activates SbGhd7 evening peak

edges.mechanism: PhyB (Ma3) is required for evening-light activation of SbPRR37 in long days; loss of PhyB eliminates the evening expression peak of SbPRR37.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days by Activating Expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: PhyB is required for light activation of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7 expression in the evening of long days.
evidence.claim: Phytochrome B activates SbPRR37 expression in the evening of LD

edges.mechanism: PhyB activates SbGhd7 expression through evening-phase light signaling in long days.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days by Activating Expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sorghum phytochrome B inhibits flowering in long days by activating expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7, repressors of SbEHD1, SbCN8 and SbCN12.
evidence.claim: PhyB activates SbGhd7 expression in LD

edges.mechanism: PhyB represses SbCN15 (SbFT1 = Hd3a-subclade florigen) via a mechanism that is PRR37/Ghd7-independent and photoperiod-independent. Loss of PhyB causes ~60-fold upregulation of SbCN15.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days by Activating Expression of SbPRR37 and SbGHD7
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: PhyB mediated repression of SbCN15 expression occurs regardless of photoperiod.
evidence.claim: PhyB represses SbCN15/SbFT1 constitutively

edges.mechanism: PhyB/PhyC mediate SbCO protein destabilisation in LD daylight hours, reducing CO activity (opposite of Arabidopsis); in SD this is released.

evidence.title: CONSTANS is a photoperiod regulated activator of flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Yang S, Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Klein PE, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Reduced PhyB/C-mediated degradation of SbCO in SD, relative to LD.
evidence.claim: PhyB promotes SbCO protein degradation in LD

edges.mechanism: PhyB inactivates ELF3 post-translationally; in rice, OsPhyB inactivates OsELF3 through post-translational modifications in response to light, thereby attenuating Evening Complex repression on Ghd7/PRR37 in LD.

evidence.title: The evening complex integrates photoperiod signals to control flowering in rice
evidence.authors: Andrade L, Lu Y, Cordeiro A, Costa MMR, Wigge PA, Saibo NJM, Jaeger KE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsPhyB inactivates OsELF3 through post-translational modifications in response to light.
evidence.claim: Rice OsPhyB inactivates OsELF3; mechanism inferred for sorghum SbPhyB→SbELF3

edges.mechanism: Phytochrome C (Ma5) acts together with PhyB to delay flowering in LD through activation of floral repressors; PhyC-light signaling contributes to PRR37 activation.

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma5 encodes phytochrome C (phyC)...acts as a photoperiod-dependent flowering repressor.
evidence.claim: PhyC (Ma5) represses flowering via same pathway as PhyB → PRR37

evidence.title: Deleterious mutations predicted in sorghum Ma and Dw genes
evidence.authors: Thurber CS, Ma JM, Higgins RH, Brown PJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma5 = SbPhyC...Light sensing...strong flowering inhibition (dominant form required).
evidence.claim: Ma5/PhyC is a dominant flowering repressor, consistent with acting upstream of PRR37

edges.mechanism: PhyC contributes to evening-light signaling that activates Ghd7 in LD, parallel to PhyB.

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: The other photoreceptor, Phytochrome C (PhyC), is proposed as the candidate gene for the Ma5 (SbPhyC) locus.
evidence.claim: SbPhyC acts upstream of Ghd7 in the sorghum LD repression pathway

edges.mechanism: Conserved circadian oscillator: morning-phased CCA1 binds EE elements in the TOC1 promoter and represses its transcription.

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: LHY, CCA1, TOC1 (circadian oscillator).
evidence.claim: Canonical CCA1-TOC1 oscillator structure conserved in sorghum

edges.mechanism: Evening-phased TOC1 represses CCA1 transcription, completing the core oscillator feedback loop.

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: LHY, CCA1, TOC1 (circadian oscillator).
evidence.claim: TOC1 represses CCA1 in the sorghum circadian clock

edges.mechanism: LHY and CCA1 act redundantly to repress TOC1 in the morning.

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: LHY, CCA1, TOC1 (circadian oscillator).
evidence.claim: LHY contributes to morning repression of TOC1 in sorghum

edges.mechanism: Morning-phased CCA1/LHY represses GI to restrict GI expression to the afternoon/evening peak.

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: GI (circadian regulator linking to CO→FT flowering pathway).
evidence.claim: Sorghum GI is regulated by the core circadian clock (CCA1/LHY repress; TOC1 permits)

edges.mechanism: Morning-phased CCA1/LHY MYB TFs repress evening-phased genes including PRR37; canonical across Arabidopsis and grass oscillators. Conservation inferred for sorghum from the clock-gated bimodal SbPRR37 expression (Murphy 2011).

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: circadian clock modulates SbPRR37 expression under...free-running conditions.
evidence.claim: Morning clock (CCA1/LHY) gates SbPRR37 evening expression

edges.mechanism: ELF4 and LUX physically associate with ELF3 to form the Evening Complex; ELF3 serves as docking scaffold. Without ELF4/LUX, ELF3 loses repressive activity on clock output.

evidence.title: The ELF4-ELF3-LUX complex links the circadian clock to diurnal control of hypocotyl growth
evidence.authors: Nusinow DA, Helfer A, Hamilton EE, King JJ, Imaizumi T, Schultz TF, Farre EM, Kay SA
evidence.evidence_sentence: ELF3 served as a docking protein that brought together ELF4 and LUX; the biological activities of the three components peak in the evening, as does the formation of the evening complex.
evidence.claim: ELF4 and LUX assemble with ELF3 to form the Evening Complex (conserved)

edges.mechanism: Evening Complex subunit ELF3 represses morning/evening PRR genes; inferred in sorghum from rice OsELF3-1 ortholog, which negatively regulates OsPRR1/37/73/95 (Zhao 2012).

evidence.title: OsELF3-1, an ortholog of Arabidopsis early flowering 3, regulates rice circadian rhythm and photoperiodic flowering
evidence.authors: Zhao J, Huang X, Ouyang X, Chen W, Du A, Zhu L, Wang S, Deng XW, Li S
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsELF3-1 negatively regulates OsPRR1, OsPRR37, OsPRR73 and OsPRR95 expression.
evidence.claim: OsELF3 (ortholog of SbELF3) represses OsPRR37; mechanism inferred for sorghum Evening Complex

edges.mechanism: Evening Complex subunit ELF3 represses Ghd7; in rice, OsELF3 suppresses Ghd7 expression, accelerating flowering under LD. Mechanism conserved in sorghum Evening Complex.

evidence.title: OsELF3-1, an ortholog of Arabidopsis early flowering 3, regulates rice circadian rhythm and photoperiodic flowering
evidence.authors: Zhao J, Huang X, Ouyang X, Chen W, Du A, Zhu L, Wang S, Deng XW, Li S
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsELF3-1 suppresses a flowering repressor grain number, plant height and heading date 7 (Ghd7) to indirectly accelerate flowering under long-day (LD) conditions.
evidence.claim: OsELF3 represses Ghd7; conserved in sorghum

evidence.title: The evening complex integrates photoperiod signals to control flowering in rice
evidence.authors: Andrade L, Lu Y, Cordeiro A, Costa MMR, Wigge PA, Saibo NJM, Jaeger KE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsELF3 directly represses the expression of the LD-dependent flowering repressors Ghd7 and OsPRR37 within the evening complex.
evidence.claim: OsELF3 directly represses Ghd7 within the Evening Complex

edges.mechanism: Evening Complex DNA-binding subunit LUX targets promoters of morning/evening PRR genes; rice OsLUX recruits OsELF3-1 and OsELF4s to repress Hd1 and Ghd7. Mechanism inferred for sorghum LUX ortholog.

evidence.title: The evening complex integrates photoperiod signals to control flowering in rice
evidence.authors: Andrade L, Lu Y, Cordeiro A, Costa MMR, Wigge PA, Saibo NJM, Jaeger KE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: The evening complex target suppresses the expression of Hd1 and Ghd7 directly to modulate photoperiodic flowering in rice.
evidence.claim: Rice OsLUX/Evening Complex represses Hd1 and Ghd7; mechanism inferred for SbLUX→SbPRR37

edges.mechanism: LUX binds the Ghd7 promoter as part of the Evening Complex; rice evidence (OsLUX recruits ELF3/ELF4 to repress Ghd7).

evidence.title: The evening complex integrates photoperiod signals to control flowering in rice
evidence.authors: Andrade L, Lu Y, Cordeiro A, Costa MMR, Wigge PA, Saibo NJM, Jaeger KE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: Evening complex target suppresses the expression of Hd1 and Ghd7 directly.
evidence.claim: OsLUX/EC directly represses Ghd7; conserved in sorghum

edges.mechanism: SbGhd7 binds the SbELF3 promoter at the TGAATG motif and directly represses its transcription (Tadesse 2024 ChIP-seq + DAP-seq).

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition and directly represses genes crucial for flowering activation
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: repressing transcription of...EARLY FLOWERING 3 (SbELF3).
evidence.claim: SbGhd7 directly represses SbELF3

edges.mechanism: SbGhd7 directly represses SbFKF1 (ChIP-seq target, TGAATG binding motif).

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition and directly represses genes crucial for flowering activation
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: repressing transcription of...FLAVIN-BINDING, KELCH REPEAT, F-BOX1 (SbFKF1).
evidence.claim: SbGhd7 directly represses SbFKF1

edges.mechanism: GI is required for normal diurnal SbPRR37 expression; sbgi-ems1 loses 2-5 fold PRR37 abundance across key time points.

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbPRR37 transcript...levels in sbgi-ems1 plants were diminished by 2- to 5-fold at these time points.
evidence.claim: SbGI activates SbPRR37 expression

edges.mechanism: GI-FKF1 complex stabilizes CO and promotes CO transcription at 12 h after dawn, coincident with SbGI peak expression.

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: The major effect of sbgi-ems1 was diminishment of SbCO transcript levels at 12 hr after dawn in either photoperiod.
evidence.claim: SbGI activates SbCO expression

evidence.title: CONSTANS is a photoperiod regulated activator of flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Yang S, Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Klein PE, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: In long days, GI activates CONSTANS (CO) expression in conjunction with FKF1.
evidence.claim: GI activates CO expression

edges.mechanism: GI activates Ehd1 expression, mainly under SD at ZT12 (SD peak strongly attenuated in sbgi).

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: The peak of SbEHD1 occurring at 12 hr under SD was significantly reduced in sbgi-ems1.
evidence.claim: SbGI activates SbEhd1 expression

edges.mechanism: Blue-light-activated FKF1 forms a complex with GI that stabilizes CO protein and promotes CO transcription at late afternoon.

evidence.title: CONSTANS is a photoperiod regulated activator of flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Yang S, Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Klein PE, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: In long days, GI activates CONSTANS (CO) expression in conjunction with FKF1.
evidence.claim: FKF1 activates CO together with GI

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbGI physically interacts with the sorghum FKF1-like (SbFFL) protein, and the SbGI-SbFFL interaction is stimulated by blue light.
evidence.claim: SbFFL/FKF1 acts with SbGI to activate CO

edges.mechanism: Ma2 locus (Sobic.002G302700, SMYD lysine methyltransferase) increases SbPRR37 expression amplitude in LD. Loss of Ma2 (ma2 = L141*) reduces PRR37 peak amplitude, advancing flowering.

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor
evidence.authors: Casto AL, McKinley BA, Yu KMJ, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma2 delayed flowering in long days by selectively enhancing the expression of SbPRR37 (Ma1) and SbCO.
evidence.claim: Ma2 activates SbPRR37 expression

edges.mechanism: Ma2 activates SbCO expression in LD; loss of Ma2 reduces both SbCO expression peaks.

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor
evidence.authors: Casto AL, McKinley BA, Yu KMJ, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: enhanced expression of both SbPRR37 (Ma1) and SbCO by Ma2 in Ma1Ma2 genotypes.
evidence.claim: Ma2 activates SbCO expression

edges.mechanism: SbID1 establishes the capacity for SbCO expression; sbid1-2 mutants lose wildtype SbCO waveform across all time points.

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: floral activator SbCO transcript accumulation in sbid1-2 was low at all time points and lacked the wild-type waveform.
evidence.claim: SbID1 activates SbCO expression

edges.mechanism: SbID1 required for proper SbPRR37 diurnal expression; sbid1-2 abolishes PRR37 peaks.

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbPRR37 and SbGhd7 transcripts were low at all time points in sbid1-2.
evidence.claim: SbID1 activates SbPRR37 expression

edges.mechanism: SbID1 required for proper SbGhd7 diurnal expression; primary Ghd7 peak at ZT16 is absent in sbid1-2.

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: primary SbGhd7 peak at ZT16 in the wild type...did not occur in sbid1-2.
evidence.claim: SbID1 activates SbGhd7 expression

edges.mechanism: SbID1 (maize ID1 / rice Ehd2 ortholog) activates SbEhd1 expression. In rice, Ehd2 up-regulates Ehd1 under both SD and LD; ehd2 mutants show 145-365 day flowering delays.

evidence.title: Ehd2, a Rice Ortholog of the Maize INDETERMINATE1 Gene, Promotes Flowering by Up-Regulating Ehd1
evidence.authors: Matsubara K, Yamanouchi U, Wang ZX, Minobe Y, Izawa T, Yano M
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ehd2 up-regulates the transcription of Ehd1 and of the downstream FT-like genes, Hd3a and RFT1, under both SD and LD conditions.
evidence.claim: Rice Ehd2 (= sorghum SbID1 ortholog) activates Ehd1; conserved in sorghum

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbid1-2 shows loss of SbCO waveform and reduced florigen expression, consistent with Ehd1 axis disruption.
evidence.claim: SbID1 is required for downstream florigen induction (consistent with SbID1→SbEhd1 via the rice Ehd2 ortholog mechanism)

edges.mechanism: SbID1 required for SbCN8/SbFT8 florigen expression peaks.

evidence.title: Photoperiod Control of Gibberellin Levels and Flowering in Sorghum
evidence.authors: Foster KR, Morgan PW
evidence.evidence_sentence: under inductive 10-h photoperiods, the peak of GA20 and GA1 levels was shifted to an early morning peak, and flowering was hastened.
evidence.claim: Gibberellin peak timing correlates with accelerated flowering in sorghum SD

edges.mechanism: Direct integrative effect of SbID1 on flowering time via activation of SbCO, SbEhd1, SbFT8 (SbCN8), and SbFT10 (SbCN12). In the algebraic framework the three-florigen cascade is diluted by geometric-mean activation and rescued by bounded-inverse inhibition when PRR37/GHD7 co-drop; adding a direct short-cut edge captures the empirical 170-day flowering delay of sbid1-2 that is not faithfully transmitted through the multistep cascade. This is a modeling short-cut per REFINEMENT_AGENT Section 1.5 Step C, not a novel biological claim.

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbid1-2 (Q199*) delays flowering ~170 days; SbCO transcript accumulation in sbid1-2 was low at all time points; SbCN8 and SbCN12 florigen transcripts reduced.
evidence.claim: SbID1 is a primary positive regulator of flowering time; sbid1-2 null causes a ~170-day flowering delay, a quantitatively large direct effect that warrants a short-cut edge to the phenotype.
